titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles A. Reich Papers
titlestmt.subtitle: Charles (Reich) Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Robert Bartels

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 29, 2024 at 05:44

did.abstract: The collection consists of the professional and personal papers of attorney, law professor, and author Charles A. Reich. Materials include Reich's writings and contemporary reactions and responses to them, in particular his 1970 book The Greening of America ; talks and lectures by Reich; interviews and profiles of Reich and his work; personal and professional correspondence; notes compiled by Reich relating to his writings; personal files concerning Reich's early life; and files relating to Reich's teaching at Yale University. The collection also contains photographs of Reich and his friends and family, as well as audiovisual material, including recorded interviews and lectures.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Chares A. Reich Papers (MS 2118). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of the professional and personal papers of attorney, law professor, and author Charles A. Reich. Writings include scholarly articles by Reich regarding legal theory; commenting on legal issues of the day; court case briefs; book reviews and proposals; and transcripts of talks and lectures given on legal topics. Also included are materials related to books authored by Reich, in particular materials on the creation, development, publishing, public responses, and critical reviews and retrospectives of The Greening of America .

The collection also contains interviews with and profiles on Reich, mostly relating to The Greening of America and the public reaction to his ideas and work; some personal and professional correspondence with friends and colleagues; notes written by Reich in preparation for his writings and lectures, in particular articles on Supreme Court Justices Hugo Black, William O. Douglas, and Felix Frankfurter; files relating to Reich's early life including schoolwork; and Yale University teaching and student files.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Yale University related files are restricted until 2057 as established by Yale Corporation regulations. Student records are restricted until 2097 as established by Yale Corporation regulations. http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Alice Reich and Daniel H. Reich, 2020.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Charles A. Reich were transferred to Yale University in 2020. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact beinecke.library@yale.edu. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Alan Reich was born in New York City in 1928. He graduated from Oberlin College in 1949 and attended the Yale Law School. During his time at Yale, Reich served as the editor-in-chief of the Yale Law Journal . After graduating in 1952, Reich served as a clerk for United States Supreme Court Justice Hugo Black from 1953 to 1954; later also befriending Justice William O. Douglas. After working as a lawyer for six years, Reich accepted an offer to join the Yale Law School faculty in 1960. He served as a professor from 1960 to 1974, teaching constitutional and administrative law, as well as authoring many legal articles, including The New Property , an examination of government-created wealth and its effects on individuals. Reich left Yale in 1974 and moved to San Francisco, California, having become fascinated by the youth counterculture movement there. While in San Francisco, he continued to write and teach law at the University of California, Santa Barbara and the University of San Francisco. Reich returned to Yale as a visiting professor from 1991 to 1994. Reich continued to live in San Francisco and died there in 2019.

Reich authored of several books. His most well-known work was his 1970 book The Greening of America , an examination and appreciation of the counterculture movement in the 1960s. The book forecasts what Reich saw as a fundamental shift in American society away from the worldviews of the 19th and early 20th centuries and into the worldview of the 1960s counterculture. The book was a success and elevated Reich into a position as a public intellectual, while also garnering criticism for his predictions and admiration for the counterculture movement. Reich also authored Garcia: A Signpost to New Space (1972) consisting of an Rolling Stone interview with himself and musician Jerry Garcia; the autobiographical work The Sorcerer of Bolinas Reef (1976); and Opposing the System (1995), examining what he saw as a societal breakdown in the twenty-five years since the publication of The Greening of America .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged in nine groupings: Writings; Interviews, profiles and talks; Correspondence; Notes; Early life and biographical; Yale University files; Photographs; Audiovisual materials; and Computer files.

did.unittitle: "New York's New Indeterminate Sentence Law for Sex Offenders"
"Samuel Roth vs. United States" obscenity brief
"Parr vs. United States" brief for petitioners
Statement before the Subcommittee on Constitutional Rights of the Senate Judiciary Committee regarding wiretapping and eavesdropping
"Another Such Victory: The President's Short War Against Steel", including reactions
"Mr. Justice Black and the Living Constitution"
"Midnight Searches and the Social Security Act"
"Source Materials: Searching Homes of Public Assistance Recipients"
"Individual Rights and Social Welfare: The Emerging Legal Issues"
"The Tragedy of Justice in Billy Budd"
Black, Hugo, photocopied manuscript notes on The Greening of America
The New York Times unpublished op-ed draft
Original typescript draft and early outline
Del Valle High School, Walnut Creek, California
Galley proofs for unpublished New Yorker review
Garcia: A Signpost to New Space and A Stoned Sunday Rap
"The Turning of America," The New York Times article
"Bureaucracy and the Forests Second Edition: A Precious Resource"
Preface for Jim Brogan's The American Search for Identity anthology
"80 Americans Appeal to India to Restore Fundamental Rights," Charles Reich a signatory
New York Post book and author column
Quotations taken from The Sorcerer of Bolinas Reef
"Beneath the Bar Facades: A Look At Why We Don't Connect"
"Mr. Justice Black as the One Who Saw the Future"
"Pro and Con: Are We Too Permissive With Our Kids?," Family Weekly
"The Party of the Withdrawn," The New York Times op-ed
Quotations for Border Crossings by Daniel Peters
"Deconstructing the Yale Realists," review of Legal Realism at Yale
Blurbs for Jack and Jim by Jim Brogan
"Beyond the New Property: An Ecological View of Due Process"
"The Liberty Impact of the New Property"
The Judicial Response to the New Deal: U.S. Supreme Court and Economic Regulation, 1934-1936, book review
From Property to Economic Rights: The Path of Constitutional Transformation, unpublished article
Comment on No Mercy by Jean Stefancic and Richard Delgado
"Property Law and the New Economic Order"
Interviews and profiles of Charles Reich
"Protest Has Value to New Yale Hero," The New York Times
"The Saint of New Haven," The Washington Post
"Charles Reich, Kids, and the New Consciousness," The Washington Post
"The Man Who Wrote The Greening," The Reporter Dispatch
"Author Says Societal Changes Must Begin at the Personal Level"
"Charles Reich: The Greening Goes On," The Washington Post
"Brainwashing Away Our Rights," Los Angeles Times
"Reich Calls Burger Court Too Political," New Haven Register
"How Charles Reich Beat the Blues," Juris Doctor
"The Greening of Charles Reich," San Francisco Chronicle
Excerpt from New Dimensions radio program
"Author Reich Urges Society to Pursue Human Values," Santa-Barbara News-Press
"Can Romance Survive the Seventies?" The San Francisco Bay Guardian
"Reich's View of the '80s: Decade of Community," Oakland Tribune
"He Can See America Greening," San Jose Mercury News
"Reich emerges from 'Greening of America'," Santa Barbara News-Press
"Fame: The Blaze and the Burnout," The Washington Post
"What a Long, Strange Trip It's Been," Connecticut Law Review
"A Prodigal Professor Goes Home," The Recorder
"Code Green: A Call to Revive America," The Forum
"The Greening of America: Phase Two," Human Rights
"The Greening of Charles Reich," Boston Globe
"Greening Author Still Waiting for America to Change," The City Star
"Reich Warns Dems" speech, Yale Daily News
Master's Tea appearance at Ezra Stiles College, Yale University
Law and Social Change lecture, University of California, Santa Barbara
"The Liberal Odyssey 1930-1980," University of California, Santa Barbara
"He Shall Not Pass This Way Again" edited transcript
Remarks at Yale Law School ceremony
The Next Stage of Social Justice advertisement
Black, Hugo, and William O. Douglas, regarding articles written by Charles Reich
McKeown, Margaret, regarding William O. Douglas article
University of California, Santa Barbara, correspondence and teaching files
University of San Francisco School of Law

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists primarily of notes made in preparation for articles or lectures written by Charles Reich, including articles on United States Supreime Court Justices Hugo Black, William O. Douglas, and Felix Frankfurter, and notes on The Wealth of Human Beings, a series of four lectures delivered at Yale University in 2000.

did.unittitle: Notes on manuscript drafts and outlines
Notes on manuscript drafts and outlines
The Wealth of Human Beings notes
Biography of Charles Reich, Current Biography
Certificate of election to the Yale Law Journal Board of Editors
Certificate of recognition from the United States Supreme Court
Horace Mann-Lincoln School of Teachers College student yearbook
The Storm, City and Country School
Libretto for the play Life of Galileo , City and Country School
The Wind, City and Country School
"For Ours are the Coming Years," Lincoln School class of 1942
"Jones and the Beanstalk," The Axon , Horace Mann-Lincoln School
"Battlefield," a radio script written by the 11th grade, Horace Mann-Lincoln School
"Economic Mythology and the Judicial Mind 1900-1930," Oberlin College
Yale Law School 50th reunion booklet and directories

Boston Globe photograph of Charles Reich

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

#1703, Hitch Your Wagon to a Star with Charles Reich

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the lectures "No. 1: The Great Disconnection: The Separation of Wealth From Life" and "No 3: The Right to Life"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the lecture "No. 4: Law and Economics in an Ecological Age"

The Wealth of Human Beings lectures
"The Great Disconnection: The Separation of Wealth from Life"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was imaged or captured by Beinecke staff in 2024 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.
